subject: Upgrade CBS java SDK to support SSL
commit_message:
Upgrade CBS java SDK to support SSL

 - add TrustStoreKeys class for one-way TLS for CBS client
 - use trust.jks & trust.pass
 - add unit test
 - top up version of Vavr lib (due to bug)

Issue-ID: DCAEGEN2-1552
Signed-off-by: Piotr Wielebski <piotr.wielebski@nokia.com>
Change-Id: I372c559cce5db8eba5448d99e12cdf6609c40d00

Patch Set 15:

(3 comments)

This change is dependent on components set use_tls to true in blueprint. As we discussed few weeks back, the use_tls was meant for application providing HTTPS as a server component. 

Either we change that notion/design around use_tls or we update the k8s plugin to distribute the trust.jks file (in addition to cacert.pem)

Patch Set 15: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

Piotr, Damian - Based on discussion on 10/24 meeting (https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/2019-10-24+DCAE+Meeting+Notes), k8s plugin enhancement was identified to support trust.jks distribution. Any timeline on when that will be supported?

Patch Set 17: Code-Review+1

Thank Piotr for the updates; looks good to me. 

Jack - This change depends on trust.jks (and trust.pass) being exposed via K8s plugin (inaddition to cacert.pem file) for supporting CBS HTTPS interface. Any concern moving forward with this change?

Patch Set 17:

Does this require a change to tls-init-container to add the trust.jks and trust.pass?

Patch Set 17:

> Patch Set 17:
> 
> Does this require a change to tls-init-container to add the trust.jks and trust.pass?

To answer my own question:  no, this doesn't require a change to the tls-init-container (which already populates trust.jks and trust.pass), but it will require a non-trivial change to the k8s plugin.

Patch Set 17:

Is this change forcing Java applications to rely on JKS for certificate storage?  My understanding is that this is deprecated in favor of using PKCS12.

Patch Set 17:

Jack - yes. Piotr reported issues to use PKC12 hence this solution relying on JKS currently. Piotr - can you clarify the original problem/error noted?

Patch Set 17:

> Patch Set 17:
> 
> Jack - yes. Piotr reported issues to use PKC12 hence this solution relying on JKS currently. Piotr - can you clarify the original problem/error noted?

The issue was related to usage of cacert.pem (I was not able to establish a connection via TLS due to SSL handshake error. 

Then I used JKS files because we are using them in production (PRH flow). 

I haven't checked PKCS12 certs.

> I suggest merging this patchset related to JKS. When PKCS12 will be available I will reimplement logic (no big deal) and check if they are going to work in our lab.

If a container is acting only as a client, we don't use the tls-init-container, and we mount a volume containing only the cacert.pem.
If a container acts as a server, we run the tls-init-container and it populates the cert directory with pkcs12 stores, jks stores, and server certs.

I have some qustions regarding this cacert.pem approach.

 1) Will this solution requier creating a Truststore via the Keytool?
 2) Will this solution requier importing cacert.pem to a Trustore by using the Keytool?

> > If a container acts as a server, we run the tls-init-container and it populates the cert directory with pkcs12 stores, jks stores, and server certs.

> I have some qustions regarding this cacert.pem approach.
> 
>  1) Will this solution requier creating a Truststore via the Keytool?
>  2) Will this solution requier importing cacert.pem to a Trustore by using the Keytool?

If a client needs a JKS truststore, then with the current implementation, it would necessary for the client to create the truststore and import cacert.pem into the truststore.   I am looking at changing the k8s plugin to provide a client with cacert.pem plus a JKS truststore plus a PKCS12 store with the CA cert.  DCAEGEN2-1938 is tracking this.
